title: clerodendrum cruentum
intro: Clerodendrum cruentum is a plant species within the Lamiaceae family, commonly known as the bloodflower or red glorybower, characterized by its vibrant red flowers and distinctive shape. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits associated with Clerodendrum cruentum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
